summary: The AI Kernel has evolved into a modular Capability and Plugin Platform. This architecture decouples core orchestration logic from specific implementation details, allowing for safer experimentation and easier extension. The universal extension model. Every major subsystem is now a Capability. - `capabilityId`: Unique identifier. - `lifecycle`: initialize, start, stop. - `contracts`: Explicit interfaces the capability implements. - `health`: Stability, latency, and status telemetry. The central runtime catalog for all capabilities. - Registration and discovery. - Contract-based resolution. - Lifecycle management. Explicit interfaces for subsystem interaction. - `IEvaluationContract`: Signal production (Maven, etc.). - `IResolverContract`: Decision authority. - `ISchedulingContract`: Resource and budget management. - `IMutationContract`: Darwin variant generation. - `IWorkspaceContract`: Semantic memory and artifact management. - `ITraceContract`: Cognitive diagnostics. | Capability | Contract | Implementation | |---|---|---| | Scheduling | `ISchedulingContract` | `KernelScheduler` |
